seo.title: Authentication & Authorization implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Authentication & Authorization projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for validating authentication and authorization systems before moving to a production environment. It focuses on session security, data isolation in multi-tenant architectures, and the hardening of identity provider integrations.

items.title: Credential Isolation
items.description: Verify that production API keys, client secrets, and database URIs are stored in environment variables and are not present in the codebase or version control.

items.title: Redirect URI Allowlist
items.description: Ensure that only production-specific callback URLs are configured in the auth provider dashboard (e.g., Clerk, Auth0, or Supabase) and that wildcards are disabled.

items.title: HTTPS Enforcement
items.description: Confirm that all authentication endpoints and callback routes are served strictly over HTTPS and that the HSTS header is active.

items.title: Provider Webhook Signature Validation
items.description: Implement signature verification for incoming webhooks from auth providers to prevent spoofed user-creation or deletion events.

items.title: Environment Parity Check
items.description: Confirm that the production environment uses a separate project/tenant in the auth provider dashboard from the staging or development environments.

items.title: Cookie Flag Configuration
items.description: Set session cookies with HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Lax (or Strict) flags to mitigate XSS and CSRF risks.

items.title: JWT Claim Validation
items.description: Verify that the backend validates 'exp' (expiration), 'iss' (issuer), and 'aud' (audience) claims for every incoming JWT.

items.title: Refresh Token Rotation
items.description: Enable refresh token rotation in the auth provider settings to invalidate old refresh tokens whenever a new access token is issued.

items.title: Short-lived Access Tokens
items.description: Configure access token TTL (Time-To-Live) to 1 hour or less to minimize the window of opportunity for intercepted tokens.

items.title: Logout Invalidation
items.description: Ensure the logout function clears the client-side cookie and triggers a server-side revocation of the session or refresh token.

items.title: Tenant ID Filtering
items.description: Verify that every database query for user data includes a 'tenant_id' filter derived from the authenticated session context, not from client-side parameters.

items.title: Middleware Role Checks
items.description: Implement server-side middleware that checks user roles (e.g., 'admin', 'editor') before executing business logic or returning sensitive data.

items.title: Horizontal Escalation Test
items.description: Manually test that a valid user from Tenant A cannot access resources belonging to Tenant B by modifying resource IDs in API requests.

items.title: Role-Signed Tokens
items.description: Include user roles or permissions within the signed JWT payload to avoid frequent database lookups for every permission check.

items.title: Default Deny Policy
items.description: Configure the authorization layer to deny access by default if no explicit matching role or permission is found for the requested route.

items.title: State Parameter Validation
items.description: Confirm the use of a unique, non-guessable 'state' parameter in OAuth requests to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery during the callback flow.

items.title: Scope Minimization
items.description: Audit requested OAuth scopes (e.g., 'email', 'profile') and remove any that are not strictly required for application functionality.

items.title: Account Linking Logic
items.description: Verify that the system securely handles cases where a user signs in with different providers (e.g., Google and GitHub) using the same email address.

items.title: Callback Error Handling
items.description: Implement error boundaries for 'access_denied' or user-cancelled OAuth flows to prevent application crashes or infinite redirect loops.

items.title: Provider Branding Compliance
items.description: Ensure social login buttons meet the brand guidelines of the providers (e.g., Apple, Google) to prevent rejection during app store reviews.

items.title: Auth Rate Limiting
items.description: Apply strict rate limits to /login, /signup, and /forgot-password endpoints based on both IP address and user identifier.

items.title: Sensitive Data Masking
items.description: Audit application logs to ensure passwords, tokens, and PII (Personally Identifiable Information) are masked or excluded from log outputs.

items.title: MFA Enforcement
items.description: Require Multi-Factor Authentication for any user account with administrative or billing-related permissions.

items.title: Audit Logging
items.description: Implement a tamper-evident audit log that records login timestamps, IP addresses, and any changes to user permissions.

items.title: AI Quota Management
items.description: Verify that AI/LLM endpoints are protected by per-user rate limits and usage quotas to prevent billing exhaustion from compromised accounts.
